_doc: TRAITS LIBRARY for Ednar's Cauldron. See CHARACTER_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md for full instructions. Each trait id (the lowercase key like 'intimidating') is referenced by name in characters.json. To add a new trait: copy any block below, paste it in, and change the values. The 'effects' object tells the game what the trait does mechanically — see the authoring guide for the full list of available effect keys.
_format: Plain JSON. Lines starting with an underscore (_doc, _note) are comments for humans — the game ignores them.
_combat_model_note: Session 4 turn-based combat: every turn each customer attacks (active uses active_attack, waiters use waiting_attack). Patience is purely a timer that ticks down each turn (no per-turn damage from patience). When patience hits 0, customer storms out and deals expire_damage. There is NO underbrew penalty.

intimidating._note: Classic angry-customer trait. Common for nobles, merchants, brutes. Stacks visibly — two intimidating customers in queue means brews need +4. This still matters even without underbrew penalty: hitting target faster = customer dies in fewer turns = you eat fewer attacks.
intimidating.name: Intimidating
intimidating.description: Brews targeting this customer need +2 damage to satisfy.

volatile._note: BOSS-TIER. Reserved for demons, mad witches, anything that can blow up in your face. Currently used by Grimdrek.
volatile.name: Volatile
volatile.description: Overbrewing this customer triggers death-throes — they retaliate BEFORE your shield/heal applies.

pious._note: Pure flavor. Doesn't do anything mechanically — just colors dialogue. Useful for tutorial-tier customers where you want a name and a vibe but no extra pressure.
pious.name: Pious
pious.description: They judge you silently. No mechanical effect.

skittish._note: For nervous, jumpy customers. Their patience drains faster WHILE ACTIVE (front of line). Pairs well with low base patience values (4-5) to make them genuinely urgent. Doesn't affect their waiting_patience_tick.
skittish.name: Skittish
skittish.description: Their patience drains faster while they are at the front of the line.

loud._note: Bram the Bard's trait. Squeezes the dice economy without breaking the always-5-in-hand rule. STUBBED in code — declared but doesn't fire yet. TODO: implement focus reduction on dice.
loud.name: Loud
loud.description: Their racket reduces your focus by 1 while they wait — you draw fewer energy points per turn.

hexer._note: Higher-difficulty trait. Disrupts your hand without breaking the structure. Best for evening/night customers. STUBBED in code — declared but doesn't fire yet. TODO: implement die rerolling.
hexer.name: Hexer
hexer.description: Each turn this customer waits, one random die in your hand rerolls to its lowest face.

draining._note: Passive composure leak — 1 composure per turn while this customer is in queue. Punishing in the late game when composure is already low. Applied separately from active_attack/waiting_attack.
draining.name: Draining
draining.description: You lose 1 composure per turn while this customer waits.

inspiring._note: POSITIVE trait. The customer helps you while they wait. Use sparingly — too many makes the game easy. Good for once-per-day surprise encounters.
inspiring.name: Inspiring
inspiring.description: All your dice gain +1 to their rolled value while this customer is in queue.

_template_for_new_traits._note: Copy this block, rename the key (e.g. 'my_new_trait'), fill in values, and move it up into the 'traits' object above. Available effect keys: brew_target_modifier (number), active_patience_drain_modifier (number), waiting_patience_drain_modifier (number), focus_modifier (number), composure_drain_per_turn (number), dice_value_modifier_global (number), overbrew_triggers_predefense (true/false), hex_die_per_turn (true/false). To request a new effect, ask Claude — adding new effect keys requires a small code change.

your_trait_id.name: Display Name
your_trait_id.description: What the player sees when they read about this trait.
